claim: Independent cross-check confirms: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements harness boundary audit with SHA256-based snapshot diff, multi-agent cross-check protocol, and evidence scope validation that blocks PRD readiness on project mutations, missing evidence results, or malformed evidence records.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot computes SHA256 digests for all project files; diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots to detect additions, deletions, and modifications
evidence.why: Cryptographic file hashing provides tamper-evident boundary audit that cannot be bypassed by agents.

evidence.summary: Boundary snapshot captured before agent execution and after completion; mutations generate BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers and force prd_ready=false
evidence.why: Runtime integration proves boundary audit is enforced, not optional.

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness iterates requiredEvidenceRoles and generates EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED blockers for any missing role results
evidence.why: Evidence that incomplete evidence chains directly block PRD readiness.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS defines explorer, cross-checker, and verifier with writes_code=false and cross_checker required_when cross_check
evidence.why: Protocol contract establishes read-only constraint and mandatory cross-check for high-risk facts.

evidence.summary: requiredEvidenceRoles returns [explorer, cross-checker, verifier] for cross_check policy and [explorer, verifier] for single_agent
evidence.why: Evidence that three-agent protocol is required for careful routing to existing projects.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE when evidence lacks project scope and PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED when project scope lacks paths
evidence.why: Scope validation prevents external URLs from satisfying existing-project fact requirements.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceDispatchPlan sets execution_policy with writes_business_code=false, project_mutation=forbidden, allowed_write_roots=[output_dir]
evidence.why: Dispatch plan declares boundary constraints before any agent execution begins.

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt includes 'Boundary: do not modify target project files. If verification would require mutation, report it in missing.' and agent_tool_profile warnings
evidence.why: Agents receive boundary constraints directly in their prompt, not only post-hoc enforcement.

evidence.summary: runDemandEvidenceDispatchRuntime returns early with status=blocked unless executeAgents=true AND allowAgentDispatch=true
evidence.why: Evidence agent execution requires dual authorization flags; dry_run is default behavior.

evidence.summary: External-only evidence test validates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker when agents cite external URLs for existing-project facts
evidence.why: Test proves scope validation blocks PRD readiness when project facts lack project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: Project evidence path locator test validates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker when project-scope evidence lacks repo-relative paths
evidence.why: Test proves concrete file locators are required for project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: Cross-check PRD readiness test validates EVIDENCE_AGENT_RESULT_REQUIRED blockers for missing cross-checker and verifier results
evidence.why: Test proves all three evidence agents must complete before cross_check PRD readiness passes.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION defines required fields: claim, confidence, evidence, assumptions, risks, missing, recommendation
evidence.why: Schema contract enforces structured evidence; missing fields result in normalization failures.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult sets status=blocked and recommendation=block when provider run fails, evidence lacks scope, or JSON is invalid
evidence.why: Malformed or failed agent results are converted to blocked status before readiness evaluation.

evidence.summary: inspectEvidenceAgreement detects conflicts when recommendations disagree (block vs proceed, or clarify/cross_check vs proceed) and adds EVIDENCE_AGENT_CONFLICT blockers
evidence.why: Cross-checker disagreement cannot be averaged away; conflicts generate blockers.

risks: If buildBoundarySnapshot does not walk all relevant files or ignores hidden files, mutations could occur undetected.
If provider-adapter executes shell commands that bypass filesystem visibility, boundary audit may miss mutations.
If evidence agents use indirect write mechanisms (symlinks, external tooling) not captured by snapshot, boundary could be violated.

result.notes: Independent cross-check confirms all explorer claims: boundary audit uses SHA256 digests, mutations trigger blockers, three-agent protocol is mandatory for cross_check policy, scope validation blocks external-only evidence for project facts, and conflicts generate blockers. No discrepancies found between explorer evidence and independent source reads.
